articles.title: Feds Sniffing Around Polymarket After Suspicious Bets
articles.summary: Federal investigators are examining unusual betting patterns on Polymarket that suggest possible market manipulation or insider trading. This probe could trigger the first major regulatory crackdown on prediction markets, potentially reshaping how these platforms operate in the US.

articles.title: Madden 27 Cover Athlete: Kalshi Predictions
articles.summary: Kalshi traders are betting on which NFL star will grace the cover of Madden 27, the latest installment of EA's flagship football video game. The market taps into both NFL fandom and gaming culture, offering sharp bettors a chance to profit from celebrity endorsement decisions that typically favor breakout stars and MVP candidates.

articles.title: Polymarket trader exploits UFC blunder, turns $676 into $67,000 in under a minute
articles.summary: A Polymarket trader capitalized on the UFC's accidental early announcement of fight results, parlaying $676 into $67,000 before the market could correct itself. The incident highlights how information asymmetries can create massive arbitrage opportunities in prediction markets, though it also raises questions about whether such wins constitute legitimate trading or exploitation of technical errors.

articles.title: Event Wagers Face $143 Million Insider Problem as War Bets Boom
articles.summary: Event betting markets are grappling with $143 million worth of wagers potentially compromised by insider trading, particularly as conflict-related betting surges. The problem threatens the core integrity of prediction markets, where information asymmetries between insiders and regular bettors could destroy public trust and invite regulatory crackdowns.

articles.title: Opinion: Congress can rein in out-of-control prediction markets
articles.summary: Someone's calling for congressional regulation to crack down on prediction markets they view as getting too wild or manipulative. Any serious regulatory push would reshape the entire industry, potentially killing offshore platforms' US access and forcing dramatic changes to how Americans bet on politics and current events.

articles.title: 'Informed' traders on Polymarket netted $143 million in 'anomalous' profit since 2024, researchers find
articles.summary: Researchers discovered that a group of "informed" traders on Polymarket made $143 million in profits this year through what they're calling "anomalous" trading patterns. This suggests either insider information is flowing through prediction markets or sophisticated traders have found exploitable inefficiencies that regular bettors are getting crushed by.

articles.title: Kalshi Approved for Margin Trading as It Lures Wall Street Pros
articles.summary: Kalshi just got regulatory approval to offer margin trading, letting users bet with borrowed money as the platform courts institutional traders. This signals Kalshi's push beyond retail bettors into serious Wall Street territory, where leveraged positions could dramatically increase trading volumes and market liquidity.

articles.title: Dan Patrick adds data centers, prediction markets and THC to Senate priorities
articles.summary: Texas Lieutenant Governor Dan Patrick is pushing legislation to prioritize data centers, legalize prediction markets, and expand THC access in the state Senate. If Texas embraces prediction markets, it could become the first major US state to fully legitimize the industry and potentially trigger a domino effect across other Republican-led states.

articles.title: California bans officials from using inside knowledge to bet on prediction markets
articles.summary: California just banned government officials from betting on prediction markets using insider information they gain through their positions. The move signals that regulators are taking prediction markets seriously enough to craft specific rules around them, which could legitimize the space but also invite more oversight.
